response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with a strong track record across web, mobile, and systems domains, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands deep JVM expertise, Java/Kotlin proficiency, and hands-on experience with the Spring ecosystem — none of which are demonstrated in his resume. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js, and while Java appears in his skills list, actual Java usage is limited to brief, non-enterprise contexts (robotic arms at Altom, NI LabWindows in C/C++). There is no evidence of IDE internals work on JVM-based tooling, no Spring/Hibernate/Quarkus exposure, and no Kotlin experience whatsoever.
strengths: Broad systems-level experience including developer tooling, low-latency infrastructure, and open source library development (Fluent-Asserts, Trial test runner)
Strong software craftsmanship ethos: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and code quality practices demonstrated consistently across roles
Experience at scale with large codebases at Stripe and Shopify, and familiarity with agentic/tooling infrastructure (MCP frameworks)
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin expertise at any meaningful depth — the primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js throughout the entire career
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience visible in any role or project
No JVM-based IDE internals experience; the one relevant IDE role (NI LabWindows) was in C/C++, lasted 6 months, and is over a decade old

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
Developer tooling and open source contributions
